title: Heritage Gold ↔ LAWIM_V2 Intent & Transaction Crosswalk

mapping_statuses.NORMALIZED_MATCH: Same concept, different representation (case, format, naming convention)
mapping_statuses.ONE_TO_MANY: One source entity maps to multiple target entities
mapping_statuses.MANY_TO_ONE: Multiple source entities map to one target entity
mapping_statuses.PARTIAL_MATCH: Partial semantic overlap; some aspects covered, others missing
mapping_statuses.EXTENSION_REQUIRED: Concept does not exist in target; requires new development
mapping_statuses.OBSOLETE: Concept no longer relevant or deliberately removed
mapping_statuses.CONFLICT: Semantics conflict between source and target

intent_model_comparison.rationale: Same semantics; Heritage Gold uses INTENT model (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE with priority/weight), current LAWIM_V2 uses project_type string field (lowercase). The 'buy' project_type encompasses the same acquisition intent.
Same semantics; Heritage Gold RENT_PROPERTY maps cleanly to project_type 'rent'. Both represent tenant-side rental intent.
Same semantics; Heritage Gold SELL_PROPERTY maps to project_type 'sell'. Both represent owner-side sales intent.
Same semantics; Heritage Gold INVESTOR_INTENT maps to project_type 'invest'. Note: Heritage Gold priority 'P0' (top-tier) differs from current 'VERY_HIGH' label convention.

Heritage Gold has explicit SEARCH_PROPERTY intent as a low-priority fallback for unqualified exploration. Current LAWIM_V2 handles this via 'other' catch-all project type but lacks the intentionality, fallback semantics, and confidence threshold logic specific to Search intent.

transaction_type_comparison.rationale: Identical semantics and string value. Both represent property rental.
Identical semantics and string value. Both represent property acquisition.

transaction_type_comparison.gold_description: Mise en vente d'un bien immobilier
transaction_type_comparison.rationale: Identical semantics and string value. Both represent property sale listing.

Heritage Gold treats short_stay as a distinct transaction type with specific qualification matrices (chambre_hotel, appartement_courte_duree). Current LAWIM_V2 has no dedicated short_stay project type; short-term rental falls under 'rent' with no formal sub-type differentiation. Extension or metadata-based sub-typing recommended.
Identical semantics and string value. Both represent real estate investment.

Heritage Gold distinguishes lease (bail commercial / longue durée 3+ ans) from standard rent. Current LAWIM_V2 has no dedicated lease type; long-term rental falls under 'rent'. The timeline_horizon field (3_months, 6_months, 1_year, 2_years) partially covers duration but does not reach 3+ years. Extension or metadata-based sub-typing needed.

Heritage Gold matrices include CESSION_BAIL transaction type for commercial lease transfers (bail commercial cession). Current LAWIM_V2 has no equivalent project type. Could be mapped to 'other' with metadata differentiation, but formal extension recommended.

Heritage Gold treats bail_commercial as a distinct transaction type in COMMERCIAL_SEARCH matrices. Current LAWIM_V2 only has generic 'rent'. Bail commercial has unique legal and qualification requirements (3-9 year terms, commercial usage rights) not captured by current model.

Heritage Gold matrices include CESSION transaction type for business asset transfers (fonds de commerce). Current LAWIM_V2 has no equivalent type. Not a pure real estate transaction but an integral part of the Heritage Gold commercial domain.

Heritage Gold has FINANCE as a distinct transaction family with 10 qualification matrices (credit_immobilier, financement_acquisition, etc.). Current LAWIM_V2 has no finance/funding project type. Financing is implicitly bundled into buy/invest projects but not modeled as a standalone request type.

Heritage Gold has FIND transaction type for professional service searches (27 matrices: agents, notaires, architects, etc.). Current LAWIM_V2 has no equivalent. This is a distinct service-discovery intent beyond property transactions.

Heritage Gold has SERVICE transaction type for 24 real estate service matrices (estimation, expertise, home staging, renovation, etc.). Current LAWIM_V2 has no equivalent. These are service-procurement intents distinct from property transactions.

intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.current_equivalent: No formal role mapping in current model
intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.rationale: Heritage Gold defines explicit role (buyer) and role type (Demandeur) per intent. Current LAWIM_V2 has no Intent model, no role mapping, and no user role abstraction tied to project_type. Roles are implicit in the project_type choice.

intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.current_equivalent: No formal role mapping in current model
intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.rationale: Same as above; tenant role not formally modeled.

intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.current_equivalent: No formal role mapping in current model
intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.rationale: Same as above; seller role not formally modeled. Note: Heritage Gold distinguishes seller (Propriétaire) from buyer (Demandeur), reflecting supply vs demand side.

intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.current_equivalent: No formal role mapping in current model
intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.rationale: Same as above; investor role not formally modeled.

intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.current_equivalent: No formal role mapping in current model
intent_to_role_mapping_comparison.rationale: Heritage Gold explicitly models unqualified visitor role as fallback. Current system has no equivalent.

detection_features: Multi-intent detection (user can express multiple intents)

intent_detection_comparison.rationale: Current LAWIM_V2 has no intent detection mechanism. Users explicitly select a project_type from a predefined list (buy/rent/sell/invest/build/other). Heritage Gold's keyword scoring, confidence threshold, multi-intent detection, entity extraction, and fallback semantics are entirely absent and require new development.
intent_detection_comparison.recommendation: Implement intent detection module with keyword scoring, configurable confidence threshold (target 0.70), FR/EN/PID language support, multi-intent detection, urgency scoring, entity extraction per intent, and fallback to a general search/other intent below threshold.

journey_workflow_stage_mapping.rationale: Heritage Gold SEARCH stage maps directly to LAWIM_V2 'search' step in buy, rent, and invest journey templates.
Heritage Gold qualification order (step 1 of 10: Intention → Type bien → ...) maps directly to LAWIM_V2 'qualification' step, which exists in ALL journey templates.

Heritage Gold MATCHING stage (offre/demande matching) is split across LAWIM_V2 search (buyer/tenant side) and promotion (seller side). No dedicated 'matching' step exists in current journey templates.
Heritage Gold VISIT maps directly to LAWIM_V2 'visit' step in buy and rent journeys. Note: sell, invest, build journeys do not include a visit step in current model.

Heritage Gold NEGOTIATION stage maps to LAWIM_V2 'negotiation' journey step AND the conversation-domain negotiation stages (inquiry → offer → counter → accepted/declined → closed). Current system has richer negotiation substate modeling than Heritage Gold. The journey step exists in buy/rent/sell but not invest (uses 'analysis','due_diligence') and build (no negotiation).

Heritage Gold TRANSACTION (execution) spans qualification du besoin → budget → délai → critères → confirmation → escalade. This partially maps to due_diligence (invest) and closing phases but is distributed across multiple LAWIM_V2 steps.
Heritage Gold CLOSURE maps directly to LAWIM_V2 'closing' step, which exists in ALL journey templates.
Heritage Gold ARCHIVING maps to LAWIM_V2 'archived' project status. Both represent terminal dossier state.

gap_analysis.summary: The Heritage Gold model is significantly richer in intent semantics, role abstraction, and transaction type diversity. Current LAWIM_V2 provides a simplified project-centric model that covers the core property transaction types (buy/rent/sell/invest) but lacks several critical dimensions.

gaps.description: No intent detection system exists in current LAWIM_V2
gaps.impact: Cannot automatically route users based on natural language input; relies on explicit project_type selection
gaps.recommendation: Implement intent detection module with keyword scoring, 0.70 confidence threshold, FR/EN/PID support, multi-intent detection, and fallback to SEARCH_PROPERTY/other

gaps.description: No formal Intent model with priority and lead score weights
gaps.impact: No lead scoring or intelligent routing based on user intent priority
gaps.recommendation: Introduce Intent model with priority levels (VERY_HIGH, HIGH, NORMAL, LOW), lead score weights, and intent-to-project_type mapping layer

gaps.description: Missing transaction types: short_stay, lease, cession_bail, bail_commercial, cession, finance, find, service
gaps.impact: Cannot represent commercial lease transfers, financing requests, professional searches, or service procurement as distinct project types
gaps.recommendation: Extend PROJECT_TYPES to include lease, short_stay, finance, find, service; handle cession_bail, bail_commercial, cession as sub-types or via metadata

gaps.description: No formal role abstraction or role mapping per intent
gaps.impact: Cannot distinguish demand-side (buyer/tenant) from supply-side (seller) roles; no role-based permissions or workflows
gaps.recommendation: Implement Role model with intent-to-role mapping, role types (Demandeur, Propriétaire, Investisseur), and role-based access control

gaps.description: No urgency detection from user input
gaps.impact: Cannot prioritize leads or projects based on expressed urgency
gaps.recommendation: Add urgency scoring to intent detection, store urgency level on Project model

gaps.impact: Cannot auto-populate qualification fields from natural language input
gaps.recommendation: Implement per-intent entity extraction (budget, location, property type, timeline) integrated with intent detection

gaps.impact: Cannot handle users with multiple parallel intents (e.g., buy AND sell simultaneously)
gaps.recommendation: Support multi-intent detection and parallel project creation for users expressing multiple intents

gaps.description: Heritage Gold defines a strict 10-step qualification order; current model has flat journey steps
gaps.impact: No structured progressive disclosure for qualification fields
gaps.recommendation: Implement ordered qualification sequence (Intention → Type bien → Ville → Quartier → Budget → Délai → Critères → Préférences → Confirmation → Escalade) with step-by-step wizard

gaps.description: Heritage Gold SEARCH_PROPERTY acts as fallback below 0.70 confidence; current 'other' project type has no fallback semantics
gaps.impact: Unclassified user queries are routed to generic 'other' without confidence-based escalation logic
gaps.recommendation: Add confidence-based fallback logic with SEARCH_PROPERTY semantics, escalation triggers, and human intervention when confidence is borderline

gaps.description: Lead score weights (50/30/60/100/25) exist in Heritage Gold but not in current model
gaps.impact: No automated lead prioritization or routing based on intent weight
gaps.recommendation: Integrate lead score weights into Project model or a separate LeadScoring service; use for agent routing and prioritization dashboards
